gene_name: ATP binding cassette subfamily B member 1
vip_summary_html: ABCB1, also known as multi-drug resistance protein 1 (MDRP1), is one of forty-nine putative members in the superfamily of human adenosine triphosphate (ATP)-binding cassette (ABC) transporters that encode transporter and channel proteins that function as efflux pumps.
vip_summary_text: ABCB1, also known as multi-drug resistance protein 1 (MDRP1), is one of forty-nine putative members in the superfamily of human adenosine triphosphate (ATP)-binding cassette (ABC) transporters that encode transporter and channel proteins that function as efflux pumps.

vip_summary_html: ADRB2 encodes the beta-2-adrenergic receptor (beta2-AR), a G-protein-coupled adrenergic receptor that plays important roles in the regulation of the cardiac, pulmonary, vascular, endocrine and central nervous systems. Beta2-AR is the target of clinically important drugs for asthma and cardiovascular conditions including hypertension and congestive heart failure.
vip_summary_text: ADRB2 encodes the beta-2-adrenergic receptor (beta2-AR), a G-protein-coupled adrenergic receptor that plays important roles in the regulation of the cardiac, pulmonary, vascular, endocrine and central nervous systems. Beta2-AR is the target of clinically important drugs for asthma and cardiovascular conditions including hypertension and congestive heart failure.

gene_name: cytochrome P450 family 2 subfamily C member 19
vip_summary_html:
CYP2C19 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics.

  • It is involved in guidelines for antidepressants, clopidogrel and voriconazole (go to list of all guidelines with CYP2C19).
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar, note that the reference sequence for CYP2C19 on hg38 is equivalent to CYP2C19*38 not CYP2C19*1.
  • the AMP tier 1 alleles are CYP2C19*2, CYP2C19*3 and CYP2C19*17 see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list
  • key variant pages are rs4244285(part of *2), rs4986893(part of *3) and rs12248560(part of *17)
  • See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM)
vip_summary_text: CYP2C19 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. gene_name: cytochrome P450 family 2 subfamily D member 6
vip_summary_html:
CYP2D6 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. It is highly polymorphic and involved in the metabolism of up to 25% of the drugs that are in common use in the clinic.

  • It is involved in guidelines for codeine and other opioids, antidepressants, and tamoxifen go to list of all guidelines with CYP2D6.
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar.
  • the AMP tier 1 alleles are CYP2D6*2, CYP2D6*3, CYP2D6*4, CYP2D6*5, CYP2D6*6, CYP2D6*9, CYP2D6*10, CYP2D6*17, CYP2D6*29, CYP2D6*41, CYP2D6 duplications and other copy number variants see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list.
  • See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM).
  • CYP2D6 is the most studied CYP for phenoconversion, where a drug-drug interaction mimics a metabolizer phenotype. paroxetine, fluoxetine and bupropion are strong inhibitors of CYP2D6 as per Flockhart Table.
vip_summary_text: CYP2D6 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. It is highly polymorphic and involved in the metabolism of up to 25% of the drugs that are in common use in the clinic. It is involved in guidelines for codeine and other opioids, antidepressants, and tamoxifen go to list of all guidelines with CYP2D6 . the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar . the AMP tier 1 alleles are CYP2D6*2 , CYP2D6*3 , CYP2D6*4 , CYP2D6*5 , CYP2D6*6 , CYP2D6*9 , CYP2D6*10 , CYP2D6*17 , CYP2D6*29 , CYP2D6*41 , CYP2D6 duplications and other copy number variants see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list . See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM). CYP2D6 is the most studied CYP for phenoconversion, where a drug-drug interaction mimics a metabolizer phenotype. paroxetine , fluoxetine and bupropion are strong inhibitors of CYP2D6 as per Flockhart Table .
citation_text: Cytochrome P450 2D6. Owen Ryan P, Sangkuhl Katrin, Klein Teri E et al. Pharmacogenetics and genomics 2009

vipSummary.html:
CYP2D6 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. It is highly polymorphic and involved in the metabolism of up to 25% of the drugs that are in common use in the clinic.

  • It is involved in guidelines for codeine and other opioids, antidepressants, and tamoxifen go to list of all guidelines with CYP2D6.
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar.
  • the AMP tier 1 alleles are CYP2D6*2, CYP2D6*3, CYP2D6*4, CYP2D6*5, CYP2D6*6, CYP2D6*9, CYP2D6*10, CYP2D6*17, CYP2D6*29, CYP2D6*41, CYP2D6 duplications and other copy number variants see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list.
  • See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM).
  • CYP2D6 is the most studied CYP for phenoconversion, where a drug-drug interaction mimics a metabolizer phenotype. paroxetine, fluoxetine and bupropion are strong inhibitors of CYP2D6 as per Flockhart Table.

gene_name: cytochrome P450 family 3 subfamily A member 4
vip_summary_html:
CYP3A4 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics.

  • It is involved in guidelines for quetiapine (go to list of all guidelines with CYP3A4).
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar
  • the AMP tier 1 allele is CYP3A4*22 and tier 2 allele is CYP3A4*20 see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list.
  • key variant pages are rs35599367 (defining for *22) and rs67666821 (defining for *20).
  • it is involved in many drug label annotations with a larger number of cautions for drug-drug interactions with CYP3A4 inhibitors (such as ketoconazole) and inducers (such as carbamazepine), see the Flockhart table for more CYP3A4 inhibitors, inducers and substrates.

gene_name: cytochrome P450 family 3 subfamily A member 5
vip_summary_html:
CYP3A5 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics.

  • It is involved in guidelines for tacrolimus (go to list of all guidelines with CYP3A5).
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar
  • the AMP tier 1 alleles are CYP3A5*3, CYP3A5*6, and CYP3A5*7 see all alleles on the AMP recommended to test list.
  • key variant pages are rs776746 (defining for CYP3A5*3), rs10264272 (defining for CYP3A5*6), rs41303343 (defining for CYP3A5*7).
  • See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM).

vip_summary_html: The 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A (HMG-CoA) reductase enzyme (EC 1.1.1.88) catalyzes the NADP-dependent conversion of HMG-CoA to mevalonate in the rate-limiting step of cholesterol biosynthesis, and is the target of the statin family of cholesterol lowering drugs.
vip_summary_text: The 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A (HMG-CoA) reductase enzyme (EC 1.1.1.88) catalyzes the NADP-dependent conversion of HMG-CoA to mevalonate in the rate-limiting step of cholesterol biosynthesis, and is the target of the statin family of cholesterol lowering drugs.

gene_name: solute carrier organic anion transporter family member 1B1
vip_summary_html:
SLCO1B1 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. The gene encodes the OATP1B1 transporter.

  • It is involved in guidelines for statins including CPIC guidelines for atorvastatin, simvastatin, and rosuvastatin (go to list of all guidelines with SLCO1B1).
  • the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar
  • key variant pages are rs4149056 also known as c.521T>C (found in SLCO1B1*5 and SLCO1B1*15 and other alleles) and rs2306283 (also known as c.388A>G and part of *15).
  • See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM).
vip_summary_text: SLCO1B1 is one of the key pharmacogenes involved in implementation of pharmacogenomics. The gene encodes the OATP1B1 transporter. It is involved in guidelines for statins including CPIC guidelines for atorvastatin , simvastatin , and rosuvastatin ( go to list of all guidelines with SLCO1B1 ). the nomenclature has been set by PharmVar key variant pages are rs4149056 also known as c.521T>C (found in SLCO1B1*5 and SLCO1B1*15 and other alleles) and rs2306283 (also known as c.388A>G and part of *15). See the Gene-specific Information Tables for Allele Definitions (for which variants comprise which star alleles), Allele function assignments (normal function, no function, decreased or increased function etc) and Diplotype-Phenotype Table (for how the alleles combine to form metabolizer phenotypes for PM, NM, UM).
citation_text: PharmGKB very important pharmacogene: SLCO1B1. Oshiro Connie, Mangravite Lara, Klein Teri et al. Pharmacogenetics and genomics 2010
